text: When registering an assistance dog, a distinction is made between policies for flights entirely outside the United States and non-stop flights to or from the United States. General advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft also applies when an assistance dog is brought into the cabin.
To ensure optimal preparation, assistance dogs should be registered for transport in the cabin no later than 48 hours before departure.

For assistance dog registration, a training certificate is required. This certificate must be from a training institution accredited by Messerli Research Institute, Assistance Dogs International (ADI), Assistance Dogs Europe (ADEu), or International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF). Alternatively, a certificate issued by a recognized training facility or a qualified dog trainer is acceptable.
In addition to a training certificate, the assistance dog must wear a clearly visible identification tag.

section: Assistance Dog Registration: Insufficient Documentation for Non-USA Travel
text: For travel outside the USA, a certificate of completed obedience training or a completed DOT Form is not sufficient for assistance dog registration.

The airline distinguishes between several categories for animal transport, including assistance dogs, animals as excess baggage, fighting dog breeds and snub-nosed animals, and animals as additional carry-on baggage. Registration options include carriage in the cabin or in the cargo hold.

section: Special Services for Passengers with Impairments
text: Lufthansa offers special services for passengers with hearing or visual impairments, or with cognitive disabilities. These services are provided before, during, and after the flight.

section: Requesting Assistance for Passengers with Impairments
text: To ensure comfort and support at the airport and on board, passengers requiring assistance should provide advance information. Assistance requirements can be registered by telephone or online after booking. When booking tickets by telephone or at a travel agency, needs should be indicated before completing the booking. It is recommended to communicate assistance requirements at least 48 hours before departure.
Passengers can register their general assistance requirements online. A link is provided for this purpose, which opens in a new browser tab.
More information and registration details for the carriage of assistance dogs are available online.
If required, airport staff will accompany passengers throughout their entire time at the airport.
Specific check-in information is available for passengers requiring assistance.
Cabin crew will provide separate, personal safety instructions to passengers who require them.

Additional information is available on various accessible travel topics, including services for passengers with sensory impairment, reduced mobility, information specific to the USA, and details for hearing, visual, and cognitive impairments.

An assistance dog, regardless of breed or type, is specially trained to perform tasks or work for a person with an impairment, which may include visual, sensory, psychiatric, intellectual, or other cognitive impairments.
Assistance dogs can be taken on any route free of charge.

For safe and undisturbed flight operations, assistance dogs in the cabin must be trained to obey commands and behave appropriately in public spaces, meaning they must not bark, growl, or jump at people or other animals. They must also not defecate uncontrollably in the cabin or at the gate; if necessary, it must be done hygienically without affecting others' health.

Lufthansa offers services for travelers with reduced mobility or sensory or cognitive impairments, aiming to ensure they feel safe, cared for, and welcome throughout their journey. These services are designed to make time at the airport and the journey on board as comfortable and accessible as possible.

To meet individual needs, passengers are requested to provide information about their assistance requirements no later than 48 hours before departure. This advance notice enables Lufthansa to provide the best possible assistance.
Groups traveling with their own mobility aids should notify Lufthansa of their care requirements as early as possible.

section: Consequences of Late or No Registration
text: If assistance requirements are registered at short notice (less than 48 hours before departure), Lufthansa will strive to provide the best possible service, but cannot guarantee it. Without advance registration, neither the requested service nor travel on the booked flight can be guaranteed.
Passengers requiring assistance should check directly with the airports on their itinerary, as many airports offer their own specific services.

To inform Lufthansa of service needs by telephone, passengers booking tickets via phone or a travel agency should communicate their individual needs before completing the booking. The contact number is 0800 8384267.
Lufthansa's online service for communicating assistance needs is available in German and English.
For online bookings, passengers can use an online form to communicate their service needs, provided the online booking is already completed.

section: How to Inform: Through a Travel Agency
text: When booking through a travel agency, passengers should inform the agency of their individual assistance needs before completing the booking to ensure the best possible service.

Lufthansa recommends registering assistance requirements in advance, as short-notice registrations may not guarantee optimal support. Passengers can register online or by calling 0800 - 83 84 267. Note that external links may have different privacy, security, or accessibility policies.

Passengers are generally free to choose a seat that meets their requirements, though safety reasons may impose restrictions. Most seats on the Lufthansa fleet have movable armrests to facilitate access.
If a passenger wishes to use a seating device, it must be checked beforehand for safety reasons. In such cases, passengers should contact Lufthansa's Medical Operation Centre.
Lufthansa can transport up to two mobility aids.

section: Services for Sensory and Cognitive Impairments
text: Lufthansa provides special services for passengers with hearing, visual impairments, or cognitive disabilities before, during, and after the flight.
Booking assistance when you book your trip helps Lufthansa provide necessary services on the ground and during the flight.

Visually impaired passengers do not require a fitness for air travel assessment. Lufthansa offers assistance such as separate briefings for delays and travel announcements, airport escort, individual safety briefings, and explanations of meal tray arrangements. Staff also assist at destinations or during connecting flights. For longer stopovers in Frankfurt or Munich, a waiting lounge is available.

Lufthansa offers hearing-impaired passengers assistance including separate briefings for delays and travel announcements, individual safety briefings, and films with German and English subtitles. Staff also assist at destinations or during connecting flights. For longer stopovers in Frankfurt or Munich, a waiting lounge is available.

section: Considerations for Passengers with Cognitive Impairment
text: Lufthansa generally carries passengers with cognitive impairments, but important considerations apply. International airports can be long and complicated, potentially causing difficulty with navigation and communication. Security, customs, and police checkpoints may induce stress and anxiety. It is crucial that passengers can follow crew safety instructions during the flight, especially in turbulence. The coping abilities of individuals with cognitive impairments should not be overestimated, as specific impairments leading to confusion or disorientation (e.g., Alzheimer's) pose a risk of passengers not paying attention to safety.
Certain requirements must be met for assistance dogs to be carried in the cabin. Detailed information on assistance dog carriage is available on a separate page.
Lufthansa staff are available to assist passengers at their destination airport or during flight changes.
The Be My Eyes app offers valuable assistance for passengers with impaired vision, making travel easier.
Lufthansa provides services for passengers with hearing impairments, acknowledging the particular challenges they may face when flying.
Passengers can register their assistance requirements online.
Information is available regarding the carriage and registration of assistance dogs.

Lufthansa provides accompaniment throughout the airport, assistance at the gate for boarding and disembarking (preferably before other passengers), and separate instructions during delays or other travel matters.
Cabin crew provide separate, personal safety instructions. A wide range of films with German and English subtitles are available onboard.

Accessible travel information covers passengers with sensory impairment, reduced mobility, hearing impairment, visual impairment, and cognitive impairment, including specific information for travel to the USA.
Flying with a hearing impairment can present particular challenges, and services are available to address these.
Assistance requirements can be registered online through the booking and reservation process.
Details are provided for the carriage and registration of assistance dogs.
Airport staff are available to accompany passengers if required.

You can transport your own (foldable) wheelchair in the cargo hold free of charge. You are permitted to take up to two mobility aids. If you are taking your own wheelchair, please specify its dimensions, weight, and special features.
At most stations, you can use a foldable wheelchair until you reach the gate. At check-in, it will receive a "Delivery at aircraft" tag and will be loaded directly at the aircraft.
If you need wheelchair assistance at your place of departure or destination, please inform us in good time, either during the booking process or afterwards.
Please note that smaller aircraft may have size restrictions for wheelchairs. A list of aircraft door dimensions by aircraft type can be found under 'Aircraft door dimensions'.

Depending on the degree to which your mobility is reduced, we offer the following services: WCHR (Wheelchair assistance to the exit at the gate). This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft and go up and down stairs, but require a wheelchair or other means of transport for long distances within the airport.
WCHS: wheelchair assistance to the aircraft.

WCHS (Wheelchair assistance to the aircraft). This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft, but cannot walk up or down stairs and require a wheelchair or another means of transport to get around the airport.

WCHC (Wheelchair assistance for a passenger who is unable to walk). This service is for passengers who are unable to walk, can only travel with their own wheelchair, and require assistance from the moment they arrive at the airport, during all phases of boarding and disembarking (including onboard), until the end of the flight and upon arrival at their destination.

For battery-powered wheelchairs, there may be safety-related restrictions and specific regulations to follow. Please complete the provided form and send it to specialcases@dlh.de to check if transport is possible.
If you are taking your own wheelchair, please note that at the airport, you will be provided with a wheelchair free of charge for use within the airport.
Lufthansa has joined the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower programme, which provides support for people with invisible disabilities.
Wearing the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower symbol is a discreet way for passengers to indicate they have an invisible disability.
Lufthansa aims to make flying as easy as possible for passengers with visual impairments. This page provides helpful tips and support to ensure a safe and comfortable trip.
Passengers can register their support requirements online via the Service Centre. Information on registering and traveling with assistance dogs is also available.

Lufthansa staff can accompany passengers throughout the airport if needed. This includes assistance with boarding and disembarking before other passengers at the gate. Separate briefings for delays or important travel announcements can also be provided upon request.
On board, cabin crew provide separate, personal safety instructions. They also offer helpful information for cabin orientation and explain the arrangement of food on the tray for clarity.
At the destination airport or during connecting flights, staff can assist passengers by accompanying them to the airport exit or looking after them until their next flight.
Passengers can register their support requirements online via the Service Centre. Information regarding assistance dogs is also available.
Lufthansa staff can accompany passengers throughout the airport as needed.
On board, cabin crew provide separate, personal safety instructions to passengers.
